behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold comparison) inside one analyzer. MOCK-MISS: no cross-module handoff is required by this behavior text. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: none; deterministic rules. Rejected higher levels because behavior explicitly scopes to the keyword-frequency analyzer’s own configured lists/threshold logic.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate analyzer with explicit positive/negative lists and thresholds; run table-driven cases with only listed words; assert exact sentiment label for each case, including boundary counts at thresholds.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated language quality and grounding to source phrases. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed explainer can be made to always include phrases, masking real grounding failures. LLM-DEP is directly triggered by 'interprets the review in context and produces natural language reasoning.' Rejected Workflow because this is a single analyzer output, not multiple agentic steps chained.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real explanation analyzer on a fixed set of reviews; assert each explanation includes at least one concrete phrase/span from the input (or close lexical variant) and fails if output is generic/template-like across diverse inputs.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic tie-break logic within the keyword-frequency analyzer. MOCK-MISS: failure does not require module interaction; it is algorithm/config resolution. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. Rejected Integration+ because behavior text is explicitly about configured tie-breaking in the scorer itself.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test mixed-keyword inputs where positive and negative counts tie (and near-tie variants), with different tie-break configurations; assert returned sentiment matches configured rule in each configuration.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm detection is semantic/pragmatic interpretation quality, which depends on real model behavior. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic mocks will not expose sarcasm-misread failures. LLM-DEP: yes, output may vary and can fail on nuanced language. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic inference step is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm-focused dataset; assert explanations classify/describe intent as negative and cite sarcastic cues (contrast between positive token and negative context). Use repeated runs or tolerance bands to account for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module wiring/contract: scorer output + explainer output composed into one response with directional consistency check. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; (b) can test consistency contract with in-process components; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected Agentic because behavior text focuses on cross-module response consistency ('returned together' and 'same sentiment direction'), not deeper explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service layer with real local scorer and explainer interface implementation (in-process), send reviews, assert response contains both fields in one payload and that extracted sentiment direction in explanation matches score label; include cases where one module errors to verify atomic response behavior.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If product intent is to validate free-form semantic agreement from a real generative explainer (not contract-level direction tagging), upgrade to Agentic.
